items.headline: FedEx launches same-day delivery with OneRail as Amazon, Walmart boost their speeds
items.summary: FedEx announced it's partnering with last-mile delivery company OneRail to bring same-day delivery to all of its customers.

items.headline: European stocks mixed as Iran war uncertainty lingers
items.summary: Shares listed in Europe struggled for direction on Tuesday, as investors continue to monitor developments in the Middle East.

items.headline: It’s like the sun exploding: One Wall Street firm fears $200 oil — and says it’s not too late for investors to prepare
items.summary: Citi sees oil prices reaching as high as $200 in a scenario where disruptions continue through the end of June.

items.headline: ‘We’re living the simple life’: I was a fisherman and my wife was a nurse. We retired with $6 million. Here’s how we did it.
items.summary: “It probably makes little sense, given our lifestyle, to obsess over retirement accounts.”

items.headline: ‘I’m completely gobsmacked’: My elderly brother has a reverse mortgage — yet he still ran out of money. Do I help?
items.summary: “My husband and I are both retired and have saved for years — we simply can’t afford to take on his financial situation.”

items.headline: Anthropic says Claude can now use your computer to finish tasks for you in AI agent push
items.summary: Anthropic and its rivals are trying to ramp up capabilities of AI agents after OpenClaw went viral earlier this year.

items.headline: Puig stock soars 15% after Estée Lauder confirms takeover talks with Charlotte Tilbury maker
items.summary: Shares of Puig Brands soared as much as 15% Tuesday after beauty peer Estée Lauder confirmed it is in talks about merging the two companies.

items.headline: How the economy would weather private-credit defaults rising to financial crisis-like levels
items.summary: A repeat of the 2008 crisis for defaults in private credit only impacts GDP growth by between one-fifth and one-half of a percentage point.

items.headline: British fintech Revolut reports record annual profit as it gears up for U.S. push
items.summary: The startup, which hit a $75 billion valuation in 2025, is one of Europe's most valuable private tech companies.

items.headline: Estee Lauder says it has a suitor. That’s still not helping its stock.
items.summary: Estee Lauder says it could merge with luxury Puig Brands of Spain.

items.headline: ‘It feels slimy’: My friend offered to be my adviser, but didn’t tell me he’s paid to push financial products. Can I trust him?
items.summary: “His revenue sharing, which he did not disclose, creates conflicts of interest as advisers may be incentivized to recommend funds that pay them more.

items.headline: Iran Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei gets another blue check on X, despite sanctions policy
items.summary: Iran's new Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, appears to have subscribed to a new X Premium English-language account.

items.headline: After one month in their custom Texas home, Simone Biles and Jonathan Owens head for Indianapolis
items.summary: The couple spent just one month living in their new Texas mansion before packing up and heading to Indianapolis, where the Owens is now set to spend the next season playing for the Colts.

items.headline: Palantir CEO Alex Karp bought a $46 million Miami mansion just before buying $120 million Colorado monastery
items.summary: Karp reportedly picked up the lavish Florida dwelling, which is in Miami Beach on a human-made island, in June

items.headline: 10-year Treasury yields edge higher as investors weigh renewed Iran war uncertainty
items.summary: The 10-year Treasury yield rose on Tuesday as renewed volatility in oil markets and lingering Middle East tensions kept investors on edge.

items.headline: Gold trades flat after early losses deepened bullion bear market
items.summary: Gold extended its slide, deepening its grip in bear market territory as investors continued to unwind positions and a strong dollar sapped demand.

items.headline: Amazon faces further AWS disruption in the Middle East from Iran conflict
items.summary: AWS is facing more service disruptions in Bahrain as a result of the ongoing conflict ‌in the Middle East, marking the second such disruptions this month.

items.headline: Asia-Pacific markets pare gains as oil rebounds on Iran war-linked uncertainty
items.summary: Asia-Pacific markets rose but pared earlier gains as oil prices rebounded, underscoring lingering uncertainty over the trajectory of the Middle East conflict.

items.headline: White House to pay TotalEnergies $1 billion to kill off East Coast wind farm projects
items.summary: The announcement comes as the Iran war disrupts global oil and gas supplies, making the U.S.' development of its LNG resources more urgent.

items.headline: EU, Australia seal trade deal as Western countries hedge against U.S. risks
items.summary: The EU and Australia agreed to a sweeping trade deal, the latest move by U.S. allies to rethink their economic ties amid deepening geopolitical uncertainty.

items.headline: Gold at $10,000? Market watchers hold firm on forecasts despite bullion bear market slide
items.summary: Gold's sharp selloff may have pushed the metal firmly into bear market territory, but some market veterans are sticking to ambitious long-term forecasts.

items.headline: Japan reports lower-than-expected core inflation for February, headline CPI eases for a fourth month
items.summary: The consumer price index fell to 1.3% last month, its lowest level since March 2022 and below the central bank's 2% target. It was down from 1.5% in January.

items.headline: CNBC Daily Open: Unusual volume spikes and skepticism drive early trade
items.summary: Global stocks pare gains after Monday's volatile session amid skepticism over the de-escalation efforts between the U.S. and Iran.

items.headline: India’s private sector growth slows to over 3-year low in March, weighed down by Mideast conflict

items.headline: CNBC Daily Open: Trump takes off-ramp on Iran ultimatum

items.headline: Brent oil prices claw back losses to top $100 again after hours
items.summary: Global oil prices edged higher in after-hours trading Monday to recoup portion of the nearly 11% lost during the regular session, as traders geared up for what’s likely to be another day driven by developments in the Iran conflict.

items.headline: Senate approves Markwayne Mullin as next DHS secretary
items.summary: President Donald Trump tapped Sen. Markwayne Mullin, R-Okla., earlier this month to replace embattled DHS Secretary Kristi Noem.

items.headline: Apollo gives investors only 45% of requested withdrawals from $15 billion private credit fund
items.summary: The withdrawals show that Apollo didn't avoid the rush of investor redemptions plaguing rivals, driven by concern over private credit loans to software firms.

items.headline: India is launching cheap weight-loss drugs — but Novo Nordisk is betting its brands will stay on top

items.headline: Estée Lauder is in talks to merge with Puig amid ongoing turnaround plan
items.summary: Estée Lauder said Monday the company is in talks to potentially merge with Spanish beauty company Puig.

items.headline: Jim Cramer says Monday’s market rally may be short-lived

items.headline: Asia wants more U.S. oil and gas to reduce Middle East dependence after Iran war, Burgum says

items.headline: Chevron CEO says Iran war impact isn't fully priced into oil market, traders have ‘scant information’

items.headline: Democrat Emily Gregory wants to represent Trump — and his Mar-a-Lago resort — in Florida's legislature

items.headline: Social Security 'doesn't allow most Americans to build wealth,' BlackRock's Larry Fink says
items.summary: Social Security is "one of the most effective poverty-prevention programs in history," but it could be improved, BlackRock CEO Larry Fink wrote in a new letter.

items.headline: Synopsys shares rally as activist Elliott builds multibillion-dollar stake in chip design firm
items.summary: Elliott did not disclose the exact value of its investment, but described Synopsys as "essential to the global chip industry."

items.headline: Trump administration plans to bring more diesel to market as fuel prices surge, Wright says
items.summary: Diesel prices have surged about 40% to $5.29 per gallon, the highest level since 2022, as the Iran war has disrupted oil supplies.

items.headline: BTS comeback draws a smaller crowd than hoped, hitting parent company Hybe's shares
items.summary: Shares in Hybe, the parent company of South Korean boyband BTS, fell 15% on Monday as their much-anticipated comeback drew a smaller crowd than expected.

items.headline: Opinion: As Trump eyes Cuba, my trips there a decade ago remind me how different things were
items.summary: Trump has threatened a 'friendly takeover' of Cuba after cutting it off from Venezuela's oil. CNBC's Justin Solomon reflects how different things used to be.
